title: Medical Route Daily Start
goal: Build one science concept block and keep English vocabulary active.

tasks.action: Study Cell Biology topic support and write three organelle functions.
Attempt Biology or MDCAT practice questions without rushing.
Revise vocabulary flashcards and mark difficult words.
Log one weak point in the error log.

coachNote: For medical students, daily consistency in Biology plus English gives better long-term retention than occasional long sessions.

title: Engineering And CS Quant Start
goal: Strengthen timed quantitative reasoning before moving to larger mocks.

tasks.action: Attempt quantitative practice under time pressure.
Open formula bank and revise weak formulas.
Use repair paths for any repeated percentage mistake.

coachNote: Engineering routes need speed, but speed should come after clean method. Check base values and units before shortcuts.

title: NAT And GAT Mixed Practice
goal: Balance verbal, quantitative and analytical reasoning in one controlled session.

tasks.action: Select tomorrow's weak topic from the results report.

coachNote: NAT and GAT preparation improves when the student rotates skills daily instead of spending the whole week on only one section.

title: International Reading And Vocabulary
goal: Improve reading purpose, central idea and vocabulary-in-context accuracy.

tasks.action: Study main idea and write the central point of one passage.
Revise vocabulary flashcards with example sentences.
Review one mistake and record the correction.

coachNote: International test students should connect vocabulary with passage purpose, not isolated word memorisation.
